caption: The video presents a slide titled "Test intuitive psych interventions: Roma," focusing on economic self-interest as part of a World Bank study. The slide compares population pyramids for two locations: Budapest, which is 99.2% Magyar, and Alsószentmárton, which is 100% Roma. Both pyramids show the distribution of males and females across different age groups, with Budapest having a more balanced and older population compared to Alsószentmárton, which has a younger demographic. The slide also highlights an intended intervention aimed at economic self-interest but notes an unintended consequence of a threat prime. A question posed at the bottom asks, "for whom?" accompanied by stick figures, suggesting a consideration of the target audience or beneficiaries of the intervention. The cursor is visible at the top right corner, indicating that the presenter may be navigating through the presentation.
